steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the text. The passage opens by establishing what Nixon is most famous for (Watergate, a scandal), then the word But signals a pivot: Nixon's legacy is complex, and additional facts follow to show a fuller picture.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — An achievement of a historical figure is described, and then reasons why that achievement is often disregarded by historians are suggested.: Watergate is not an achievement, and no reasons for historical disregard are given. ✗
  • Choice B — Information supporting a particular perception of a historical figure is provided, and then additional facts are offered suggesting that this perception is an oversimplification.: The first sentence presents the common perception (Nixon = Watergate); the But sentence adds facts showing that view is incomplete. ✓
  • Choice C — A list of historical figures is presented, followed by reasons why one should be considered more significant than the others.: No comparison of significance between multiple figures occurs. ✗
  • Choice D — An assertion about a historical figure is made, and then evidence both for and against that assertion is presented.: The passage does not present evidence against the Watergate perception — it only adds positive facts. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
